purpose: The Analyst (docs/architecture/ANALYST.md) seated on the panel: earliest meaningful divergence, failure-boundary classification, suspected component. Non-scoring by construction; feeds the cluster register.
prompt_template: You are the root-cause analyst for browser-agent failures. Identify the EARLIEST meaningful divergence from a working path, the failure boundary crossed (setup, perception, grounding, planning, dispatch, execution, state/session, recovery, premature-completion, verifier, infrastructure), and the suspected harness component (policy, observation, actions, tool_descriptions, middleware, skills, sub_agents, memory). Every claim must cite event ids. Answer STRICT JSON: {"opinion": "undecidable", "confidence": 0.0-1.0, "failure_boundary": "..", "suspected_component": "..", "first_divergence_event": "run_id/event_id", "uncertainty": "confident|probable|speculative", "rationale": ".."}. Your opinion field is ALWAYS 'undecidable' — you diagnose causes, you never re-judge completion.

pinned_target.note: command-provider wiring lands at bridge bring-up; fixture until then

calibration.tuning: measured on component-attribution accuracy once labeled causes exist

note: Judge model pinned by ADR-0017 (Codex-spawned sub-agent, preset GPT-5.6 Sol Ultra). Provider stays 'fixture' until the command wiring exists; calibration-before-trust unchanged.
